name: 06_Sentiment_Analysis_SVM_Sentiment140_ML
query: Por favor, ayúdame a construir un sistema para el análisis de sentimientos en tweets utilizando el conjunto de datos Sentiment140 disponible en Hugging Face. Carga el conjunto de datos Sentiment140 y, al cargar los datos, límpialos eliminando palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`. Usa Word2Vec o GloVe para la vectorización de texto. Esto debe ocurrir en `src/data_loader.py`. Luego, implementa y entrena un clasificador SVM en `src/model.py`. Finalmente, escribe un informe de la precisión del clasificador en `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`. Idealmente, el informe debe ser fácilmente interpretable.

requirements.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Sentiment140", disponible en "Hugging Face", se obtiene en `src/data_loader.py`.
El conjunto de datos se limpia, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`.
Se utilizan incrustaciones de palabras, ya sea "Word2Vec" o "GloVe", para convertir texto en vectores en `src/data_loader.py`.
Un "clasificador SVM" se implementa y entrena en `src/model.py`.
La puntuación de precisión se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

preferences.criteria: El proceso de descarga del conjunto de datos debe ser confiable, con un manejo claro de errores.
El informe final de precisión debe ser directo y fácil de interpretar.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Sentiment140", disponible en "Hugging Face", se obtiene en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file is a minimal placeholder implementation that does not actually load the Sentiment140 dataset from Hugging Face. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` with no actual dataset loading logic. There is no import of `datasets` from Hugging Face, no call to `load_dataset("sentiment140")` or any equivalent, and no real data retrieval mechanism. The file merely lists the requirement criteria as strings and keywords, confirming it is a stub rather than a functional implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos se limpia, incluyendo la eliminación de palabras vacías, puntuación y caracteres especiales, todo en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file contains only a placeholder implementation. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a dictionary with a hardcoded string `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`, and there is no actual data cleaning logic implemented — no removal of stop words, punctuation, or special characters. The file is explicitly described as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" with no functional preprocessing code whatsoever.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utilizan incrustaciones de palabras, ya sea "Word2Vec" o "GloVe", para convertir texto en vectores en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file is a minimal placeholder that only contains metadata strings listing "Word2Vec" and "GloVe" as keywords, but does not implement any actual word embedding functionality. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a dictionary with a hardcoded string `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}`, and there is no code that loads Word2Vec or GloVe models, trains embeddings, or converts text to vectors. The file is explicitly labeled as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging," confirming it does not fulfill the actual requirement.

judge_stats.criteria: Un "clasificador SVM" se implementa y entrena en `src/model.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/model.py` file does not implement or train an SVM classifier. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub implementation with metadata strings listing "SVM classifier" as a requirement keyword, and a `build_model()` function that returns a string placeholder ("model placeholder"). There is no actual SVM implementation (e.g., no `sklearn.svm.SVC`, `LinearSVC`, or any equivalent), no training logic, no feature extraction, and no use of the Sentiment140 dataset. The file is explicitly a "minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" — a fake stub, not a real SVM classifier.

judge_stats.criteria: La puntuación de precisión se imprime y guarda como `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/metrics/accuracy_score.txt` exists, but its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which is clearly a placeholder value rather than an actual computed accuracy score. This indicates the file was created but never populated with a real accuracy metric from model evaluation. A genuine accuracy score (e.g., a float like `0.8523`) is not present, so the criterion of saving the actual accuracy score is not satisfied.
